claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 15 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a son, Alex, who is 15 years old and lives with you.

I have a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! That's helpful.

Next question: **Are you responsible for Alex's care - meaning do you pay for things like his food, clothes, and other expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for him - I pay for all his food, clothes, school things, everything he needs really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

One more important question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You are fully responsible for his care and pay for all his expenses
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

This means you meet the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Let me provide you with the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is 15 (under 16)
- You live in the UK
- You're responsible for his care and pay for his expenses
- You live with him
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that even when Alex turns 16, you may still be able to continue receiving Child Benefit if he stays in approved education or training until he's under 20.

If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16, so he qualifies under the age criteria. He lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is fully responsible for his care, paying for all his food, clothes, and other expenses. Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 15-year-old son Alex. The claimant lives in the UK, has full responsibility for Alex's care, lives with him, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All standard eligibility requirements are satisfied.

Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
